hoses: Supply line from hydrant to Engine 181 intake
Line A: 200 ft of 2½-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to portable monitor
Line B: 200 ft of 2½-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to portable monitor
nozzles: Portable monitor with 1¾-inch smooth bore tip
sceneElements.elevation: Level parking lot operation, 0 PSI elevation correction

problems.title: Master Stream Flow
problems.question: The portable monitor uses a 1¾-inch smooth bore master-stream tip at 80 PSI nozzle pressure. Use the smooth-bore flow formula: GPM = 29.7 × diameter² × √NP. Determine the master stream flow.
formulaBreakdown: Smooth bore flow formula: GPM = 29.7 × d² × √NP.
problems.instructorExplanation: The portable monitor is using a smooth bore tip. Smooth bore flow is calculated using tip diameter and nozzle pressure. A 1¾-inch tip at 80 PSI produces approximately 815 GPM.
problems.explainMistake: Do not use handline fog nozzle flow values for this question. A portable monitor with a smooth bore tip uses the smooth bore flow formula.

problems.title: Line A Flow
problems.question: The portable monitor is flowing 815 GPM total and is supplied by two equal lines from Engine 181. The two lines split the monitor flow; they do not each flow the full monitor total. What flow should be used for Line A?
formulaBreakdown: The monitor is supplied by two equal supply lines.
Line A flow = total monitor flow ÷ number of supply lines.
problems.instructorExplanation: The portable monitor flow is divided between two equal supply lines. Line A carries half of the total monitor flow.
problems.explainMistake: Do not place the full monitor flow in Line A. Because the monitor is supplied by two equal lines, each line carries one-half of the total flow.

problems.title: Pump Discharge Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying a portable monitor flowing 815 GPM total through two equal 200 ft 2½-inch supply lines. Split the monitor flow equally between the two lines, use FL = C × (GPM/100)² × (length/100) with C = 2, add 80 PSI nozzle pressure, add 25 PSI portable monitor appliance loss, and use no elevation. What pump discharge pressure is required?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss.
Flow is split between two equal supply lines.
FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
Line A and Line B use 2½-inch hose, so C = 2.
Each supply line length = 200 ft = 2 hundreds.
Round FL to nearest 5 PSI: 65 PSI.
problems.instructorExplanation: For a portable monitor supplied by two equal lines, calculate the friction loss for one supply line using half of the total monitor flow. Since both lines are equal, either line gives the governing friction loss. Add nozzle pressure and appliance loss to get the required pump discharge pressure.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate friction loss using the full monitor flow through one hose. The total flow is split between Line A and Line B before reaching the portable monitor.
